channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How to watch Australia vs England: live stream 5th Ashes Test for *FREE* from anywhere, Day 1
item.description: All the ways to watch 5th Ashes Test live streams for FREE from anywhere, as Australia and England face off in the series finale at Sydney Cricket Ground.
item.dc:creator: Aatif Sulleyman, TechRadar

item.title: Big Tech's fast-expanding plans for data centers are running into stiff community opposition
item.description: Tech companies looking to plunge billions of dollars into ever-bigger data centers to power artificial intelligence and cloud computing are increasingly being voted down.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A California lawmaker wants to ban AI from children’s toys
item.description: A California lawmaker has introduced a first-in-the-nation bill meant to ban companies from embedding AI chatbot technology into toys designed for children.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: NDAA scales back ambitious acquisition reforms, offers little on workforce
item.description: "Without a workforce initiative, it is hard to see the workforce having the tools to take the risks the legislation wants them to take,” Stan Soloway said. The post NDAA scales back ambitious acquisition reforms, offers little on workforce first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: The 14 global trends that will shape the climate in 2026
item.description: The future of the energy transition is being shaped by President Donald Trump’s climate policy rollbacks that favor fossil fuels — but also the life support for renewables and batteries paradoxically coming from data center power demand.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Sam Altman wants to pay some lucky person $555,000 a year to look after OpenAI's AI - but obviously, all is not quite as it seems
item.description: OpenAI is hiring a new head to look after model abuse and safety for $555k plus equity, but it'll be high-stress.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: Five things to watch in cybersecurity for 2026
item.description: The White House is expected to kick off 2026 with a new national cyber strategy. Here's what else we're watching in a consequential year for cyber. The post Five things to watch in cybersecurity for 2026 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Grok makes sexual images of kids as users test AI guardrails
item.description: Elon Musk's artificial intelligence chatbot Grok created sexualized images of minors on the social media platform X in response to user prompts in recent days, drawing criticism of a tool that positions itself as less restrained than its competition.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: There's still no sign of the Nvidia RTX 5080 32GB despite rumors – and a fresh claim suggests a worrying possibility
item.description: Fresh rumors suggest Nvidia RTX 5080 32GB was initially set for cloud gaming only, and that's a worrying sign for any potential launch.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Williams, TechRadar

item.title: Apple Fitness+ is kicking off 2026 with new workout programs
item.description: Apple Fitness+ is kicking off 2026 with new workout programs aimed at encouraging users to jump into – or jump back into – yoga, strength, or HIIT.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Krol, TechRadar

item.title: Fake AI chat results are spreading dangerous Mac malware
item.description: Mac malware campaign uses fake AI chat results to deliver AMOS through terminal commands, security researchers report. Attackers exploit trust in AI-generated cleanup guides.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Opinion: EPA helping AI industry grow but ignoring technology’s power to protect environment
item.description: Agency must use artificial intelligence to defend communities with same zeal it applies to cutting protections that safeguard them.
item.dc:creator: Avi Garbow, The Mercury News

item.title: Chromium explained: How the open-source engine drives today’s browsers
item.description: 3 facts to know about Chromium Chromium provides the core engine, network stack, and architecture for Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, and other major web browsers. The project’s code lineage traces back to 2001, forking from Apple’s WebKit.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Las mejores entrevistas a líderes de TI en CIO ESPAÑA en 2025
item.description: Sonia Segade, directora de Transformación Digital y Tecnología de Renfe La apuesta por la inteligencia artificial, la hiperautomatización, el análisis de datos, la ciberseguridad y la convergencia entre las tecnologías de información y de operación son algunas de las palancas de futuro de Renfe, seg
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Space X plans to lower Starlink satellites’ orbital altitude
item.description: Space X plans to lower the orbit of all Starlink satellites in 2026, Reuters reports. According to the private space company’s chief technology officer Michael Nicolls, the orbit of the satellites will be lowered from about 550 to 480 kilometers. The aim is to increase safety in space.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cloud costs now No. 2 expense at midsize IT companies behind labor
item.description: Midsize IT companies now spend huge percentages of their revenues on cloud services, driven by a growing number of AI workloads running in the cloud, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: For Apple in ’26, it’s time to take the ‘Snow Leopard’ approach to OS updates
item.description: If the current speculation is correct, Apple’s 2026 operating system releases will follow a familiar path: they’re more likely to mimic the macOS Snow Leopard pattern set in 2009 than deliver a raft of snazzy new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to share your estimated arrival time on iPhone and Android
item.description: ETA sharing on Apple Maps and Google Maps keeps contacts updated automatically while you drive safely. Learn step-by-step instructions for iPhone and Android.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FAA ramps up billions in spending as ‘down payment’ for air traffic overhaul
item.description: The agency expects to spend more than $6 billion this year to lay the groundwork for a complete overhaul of the rapidly aging air traffic control system. The post FAA ramps up billions in spending as ‘down payment’ for air traffic overhaul first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: The AI data fabric: Your blueprint for mission-ready intelligence
item.description: Building an AI-ready data fabric requires more than simple integration. It demands systems designed for adaptability, compliance and continuous intelligence. The post The AI data fabric: Your blueprint for mission-ready intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: The top Google Pixel tips of 2025
item.description: More and more, there are Android tips — and then there are Pixel tips. Owning a Google Pixel phone has become a ticket of sorts to a uniquely top-tier type of Android experience.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Foreign tech workers are avoiding travel to the US
item.description: I go to a lot of tech conferences — 13 in 2025 — and many of those I attend are outside the US; several are in London, one is in Amsterdam, another in Paris, and two in Tokyo.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The 27 best new apps of 2025
item.description: As always, many of this year’s best apps are ones you’ve probably never heard of. Sure, there are some big names on this list, particularly in the buzzy field of artificial intelligence, but the real standouts of 2025 innovated on a smaller scale.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: AI takes on return fraud as holiday returns surge
item.description: Retailers lose $76.5 billion annually to return fraud as nearly 10% of U.S. retail returns involve fraudulent activity, with $850 billion in returns expected in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Intel stories of 2025
item.description: It’s been a roller coaster of a year for Intel, marked by the arrival of a new chief, new Xeon processors, select product divestitures, and sizable layoffs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 4 New Year’s resolutions for devops success
item.description: It has been a dramatic and challenging year for developers and engineers working in devops organizations. More companies are using AI and automation for both development and IT operations, including for writing requirements, maintaining documentation, and vibe coding.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI’s trust tax for developers
item.description: Andrej Karpathy is one of the few people in this industry who has earned the right to be listened to without a filter. As a founding member of OpenAI and the former director of AI at Tesla, he sits at the summit of AI and its possibilities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Using DISM to install Windows updates
item.description: Windows’ built-in Deployment Image Servicing and Management (DISM) command, a.k.a. dism.exe, is something of a Swiss Army knife when it comes to working on Windows OS images.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Pornhub hit by massive user data leak exposing 200 million records
item.description: ShinyHunters claims responsibility for stealing 94GB of Pornhub user data affecting over 200 million records and demands Bitcoin ransom.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 Best apps to use on ChatGPT right now
item.description: ChatGPT 2025 now connects to Apple Music, Canva, Expedia, TripAdvisor and OpenTable through built-in apps that help users create playlists, design graphics and more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
